mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals rapid labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving sustained positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with an out-of-sample accuracy exceeding 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a deceleration in the index of industrial production signals capacity constraints and margin compression, causing negative forward equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during late-cycle phases yields positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in over 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels erode real purchasing power and prompt anticipatory monetary tightening, generating immediate negative market returns over a five-day window.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL month-over-month changes correlate with positive 5-day forward returns in more than half of observed periods.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1.4 million units, it signals a severe credit contraction in the residential sector, dragging down broad equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 58 percent of months where HOUST remains below 1400 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a stable unemployment rate indicates robust labor demand absorption, supporting positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal produces a negative rank IC over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, sustained federal funds rate hikes above 4.5 percent force equity multiple compression, resulting in negative forward returns over a three-month period.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 65 percent of quarters when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5 percent during tightening phases.
